title: E2E: WASM protocol integration test
description:
## What
Write a Rust integration test in `relay/tests/` that verifies the JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol sequence the WASM client will use, exercising: connect → auth → broadcast → deliver → disconnect. This is a protocol-level proxy for the full browser E2E test.

## Algorithm
1. Create relay/tests/wasm_protocol.rs
2. Spin up a test relay (same pattern as relay/tests/stress.rs):
   - Start relay in-process with a temp SQLite file
   - Get the local address
3. Implement a minimal JSON-RPC 2.0 client in the test (using tokio-tungstenite directly):
   - connect_rpc(addr) -> (WsSink, WsStream)
   - send_request(sink, id, method, params) -> ()
   - recv_message(stream) -> serde_json::Value
4. Execute auth sequence:
   a. Receive challenge notification from relay (method == 'challenge')
   b. Generate test identity: Identity::generate()
   c. Sign challenge: auth::sign_challenge(&identity, &nonce)
   d. Send authenticate request with correct params
   e. Assert response: result.pub_id == identity.pub_id().0
   f. Assert result.sequence is a u64
5. Execute messaging sequence:
   a. Connect a SECOND client with a different identity, authenticate it
   b. Client 1 sends broadcast with payload = base64(JSON of ClearMessage::Chat { text: 'hello' })
   c. Assert client 1 receives ack with message_id (UUID hyphenated string)
   d. Assert client 2 receives deliver notification with from == client1.pub_id and same payload
   e. Assert relay did NOT modify or deserialize the payload (verify payload bytes are identical)
6. Execute disconnect: close client connections
7. Test MUST NOT modify any existing test file or break existing tests.

## Defensive programming
- Test uses real relay (no mocking), so auth and routing logic is exercised end-to-end
- Payload bytes verified bit-for-bit: relay must not modify opaque payload
- Test is self-contained: spins up relay, runs, tears down — no external services needed
- Never hardcode timeouts shorter than 5 seconds (CI can be slow)

## Agent team
Standard team (Researcher + Implementer + Tester + Validator):
- Researcher: read relay/tests/stress.rs for relay startup pattern; read core/src/auth.rs for sign_challenge.
- Implementer: write relay/tests/wasm_protocol.rs.
- Validator: cargo test -p nie-relay -- wasm_protocol exits 0.

## Tests
- cargo test -p nie-relay -- wasm_protocol::test_auth_and_broadcast exits 0
- Payload integrity assertion passes (deliver payload == sent payload)
- Auth rejection test: wrong signature returns error code -32001
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-relay -- wasm_protocol exits 0; tests cover: auth success, auth rejection (wrong sig), broadcast + deliver round-trip, payload byte-for-byte integrity
close_reason: E2E protocol integration test passing: auth success, auth rejection (-32001), broadcast+deliver round-trip, payload byte-for-byte integrity
